skill.name: emailworkflowskill
skill.description: Plan, audit, and document email lifecycle workflows with routing maps, trigger inventories, QA gates, and approval checkpoints for live-system changes.
skill.summary: Create reviewable email workflow plans with lifecycle routing, trigger ownership, and operator-ready QA checks.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged four weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the reviewed locations contain descriptive email workflow text only. No cryptographic code, command execution, network activity, data exfiltration, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt was found in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain normal skill descriptions and workflow guidance, not weak cryptographic algorithms or related implementation code. These findings are false positives because no hashing, encryption, key handling, or crypto API usage appears at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines were reviewed and contain only descriptive workflow language. There is no evidence of cryptographic algorithms, crypto libraries, encoded payloads, or executable logic.

content.value_statement: Email automations often fail because triggers, exits, owners, and suppression rules are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce reviewable workflow maps, QA plans, and approval gates.
actual_capabilities: Maps email workflow routes with triggers, waits, branches, exclusions, exits, and reporting checkpoints.
Identifies entry sources such as forms, product events, imports, webhooks, CRM stages, and manual enrollments.
Reviews trigger ownership and source-of-truth decisions across product, CRM, billing, support, and ESP data.
Flags routing collisions where contacts may receive competing messages or bypass suppression logic.
Produces handoff notes, routing diffs, collision audits, and operator-ready QA plans.
Separates analysis from live-system actions and requires explicit approval before high-risk changes.
limitations: Does not connect directly to email service providers or CRM systems.
Does not send emails, import contacts, edit DNS, or change production automations by itself.
Requires accurate workflow exports, screenshots, event lists, or operator context for precise audits.
Does not replace legal review for consent, privacy, or regional email compliance requirements.

use_cases.title: Audit Lifecycle Routing
use_cases.description: Review onboarding, activation, retention, or recovery flows for unclear triggers, missing exits, and duplicate enrollment paths.

use_cases.title: Prepare Automation Handoff
use_cases.description: Turn workflow decisions into operator-ready notes with ownership, source-of-truth fields, QA checks, and approval gates.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Message Collisions
use_cases.description: Find places where contacts can receive competing emails, skip suppressions, or continue through stale branches.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Basic Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to map an onboarding email workflow. Include entry criteria, exit criteria, waits, branches, owners, and QA checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Trigger Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this email workflow trigger logic. Identify each enrollment source, source-of-truth field, suppression rule, and reporting checkpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Find Routing Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this lifecycle map for routing gaps, duplicate entry paths, stale exits, message collisions, and unclear ownership. Return a prioritized QA plan.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a routing diff and handoff plan for this email automation change. Separate analysis from live actions and list every approval required before deployment.

output_examples.input: Audit a retention workflow where users can enter from a product event and a CRM stage update.
output_examples.output: The response includes a route map, trigger inventory, collision risks, suppression checks, reporting checkpoints, and approval gates.

output_examples.input: Prepare handoff notes for changing a welcome sequence wait step and exit rule.
output_examples.output: The response describes the proposed routing diff, affected contacts, QA steps, rollback checks, and required production approvals.

output_examples.input: Find why trial users receive both activation and sales follow-up messages.
output_examples.output: The response compares entry sources, branch rules, owner boundaries, suppression timing, and recommended fixes for review.

best_practices: Provide workflow exports, screenshots, lifecycle definitions, and examples of affected contacts.
Name one source of truth for every trigger, branch, suppression rule, and exit condition.
Get explicit approval before live sends, imports, suppression edits, DNS changes, or production automation changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to send email or change a live automation without a separate approval step.
Do not rely on vague intent when explicit product, CRM, billing, or ESP events are available.
Do not audit only message copy when the issue may be caused by routing, suppression, or ownership gaps.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, audit, and document email workflows, lifecycle routing, trigger ownership, and QA paths.

faq.question: Can it change my email platform?
faq.answer: No. It provides plans and checks. Live sends, imports, DNS changes, and automation edits require explicit approval.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide lifecycle definitions, workflow maps, entry sources, suppression rules, recent failure examples, and reporting needs.

faq.question: Does it support compliance review?
faq.answer: It can flag consent and suppression checkpoints, but it does not replace qualified legal or compliance review.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Lifecycle marketers, marketing operations teams, product growth teams, and agencies managing email automation can use it.

faq.question: What outputs can I expect?
faq.answer: Typical outputs include workflow maps, trigger inventories, routing diffs, collision audits, handoff notes, and QA plans.
